label: Participant ID (ADNI roster ID)
recommended_downstream_handling: use as the participant key; one row per RID

label: Participant ID (human-readable)
recommended_downstream_handling: display only

label: Plasma anchor date (survival time origin)
derivation: earliest eligible plasma draw (>=1 usable core assay) whose nearest diagnosis within +/-90d is MCI
recommended_downstream_handling: use only to recompute offsets; the duration column is already derived
known_caveat: later or more complete draws are NEVER preferred

label: Visit code of the anchor plasma draw
recommended_downstream_handling: QC only

label: Source row of the selected plasma draw
derivation: 0-based positional index of the record in the raw CSV as delivered, stamped immediately after read_csv and BEFORE any filtering/sorting/dedup
recommended_downstream_handling: use to trace the anchor back to the raw plasma CSV
known_caveat: the UPENN plasma panel has NO native row id — this generated index is the only one

label: Date of the MCI diagnosis matched to the anchor
derivation: nearest MCI diagnosis within +/-90d of the anchor
recommended_downstream_handling: QC only
known_caveat: may fall AFTER the anchor (negative align_offset_days)

label: Visit code of the matched MCI diagnosis
recommended_downstream_handling: QC only

label: Source row of the matched MCI diagnosis
derivation: 0-based positional index of the record in the raw CSV as delivered, stamped immediately after read_csv and BEFORE any filtering/sorting/dedup
recommended_downstream_handling: trace the matched MCI diagnosis to the raw DXSUM CSV

label: Native ADNI row id of the matched MCI diagnosis
recommended_downstream_handling: alternative trace key

label: Signed plasma-to-diagnosis gap (anchor - matched dx)
recommended_downstream_handling: review large |offset| in QC; negative = the MCI diagnosis came AFTER the draw

label: Absolute plasma-to-diagnosis gap
recommended_downstream_handling: 26 participants sit in the 70-90d band

label: Plasma draw and MCI diagnosis on the same day
recommended_downstream_handling: QC only

label: Same-day vs cross-visit anchor match
recommended_downstream_handling: QC only

label: Diagnosis at baseline
derivation: MCI by construction — the anchor requires an MCI match
recommended_downstream_handling: constant; confirms every participant is MCI at baseline

label: Count of usable core plasma assays at the anchor draw
derivation: count of non-sentinel, positive core assays
recommended_downstream_handling: the broad anchor requires >=1

label: Documented anchor tie-break
recommended_downstream_handling: documentation only

label: Age at study entry — THE authoritative age variable
timing_rule: UNDATED — no datestamp exists in the source; age-at-anchor is NOT validly reconstructable
missing_value_rule: no imputation; 0 missing in the survival cohort
recommended_downstream_handling: use as the age term in Model 0 and Model 1, exactly as provided
known_caveat: UNDATED. It is age at STUDY ENTRY, not at the anchor; the anchor can be years later (median 0.14y, p90 7.1y). No source date or timing offset was fabricated. Do NOT substitute age_at_anchor_approx.

label: Source row of the entry_age record
derivation: 0-based positional index of the record in the raw CSV as delivered, stamped immediately after read_csv and BEFORE any filtering/sorting/dedup
recommended_downstream_handling: trace to the raw My_Table CSV

label: Date of the age measurement
derivation: structurally absent — My_Table carries no date column
recommended_downstream_handling: do not populate; the absence is the finding
known_caveat: deliberately empty. NO date was fabricated.

label: Does entry_age carry a datestamp?
recommended_downstream_handling: documentation only
known_caveat: always FALSE — this is the age limitation in one column

label: PROXY study-entry date (earliest dated DXSUM visit)
derivation: earliest dated diagnosis visit per participant
recommended_downstream_handling: QC only — NOT an age date
known_caveat: an ASSUMPTION, not documented in the source. Never treat as the entry_age date.

label: Years from the entry proxy to the anchor
recommended_downstream_handling: use ONLY to judge how stale entry_age is for a participant
known_caveat: 28 participants have a NEGATIVE value (anchor precedes the entry proxy) -> age_derivation_suspect

label: APPROXIMATE age at anchor — NOT AUTHORITATIVE
recommended_downstream_handling: DO NOT USE AS THE MODEL'S AGE VARIABLE
known_caveat: rests on an undocumented assumption (entry_age is measured at the earliest DXSUM visit). Provided for transparency only. Use entry_age.

label: Flag: age is entry_age, not age-at-anchor
recommended_downstream_handling: documentation only
known_caveat: always TRUE

label: Flag: anchor precedes the study-entry proxy
recommended_downstream_handling: NOT an exclusion; review if age is ever re-derived

label: APOE genotype string
recommended_downstream_handling: source of APOE4_COUNT; keep for audit

label: Number of APOE e4 alleles
derivation: count of the character '4' in GENOTYPE
missing_value_rule: NO IMPUTATION. Missing for 9 of the 410 survival participants — those 9 are EXCLUDED from the primary cohort (this is the entire 410->401 drop; 1 of them is an event)
transformation_allowed: YES — treat as linear count or as a factor; that is a modeling-stage decision
recommended_downstream_handling: use in Model 0 and Model 1. Nonmissing for all 401 primary participants.
known_caveat: 0 alleles in 215 primary participants — a legitimate value, not missing

label: Source row of the APOE record
derivation: 0-based positional index of the record in the raw CSV as delivered, stamped immediately after read_csv and BEFORE any filtering/sorting/dedup
recommended_downstream_handling: trace to the raw APOERES CSV

label: Native ADNI row id of the APOE record
recommended_downstream_handling: alternative trace key

label: Plasma p-tau217 — THE primary biomarker
derivation: raw assay value at the anchor draw; sentinels and non-positive values -> NaN
timing_rule: measured AT the anchor draw (offset = 0 by construction)
missing_value_rule: ZERO missingness in the survival cohort after the existing validity rules (0 sentinels existed)
transformation_allowed: YES — log-transform / scaling are MODELING-STAGE decisions, deliberately NOT applied here
recommended_downstream_handling: the added term in Model 1. Distribution is right-skewed; a log transform is likely but is Person 2's call.
known_caveat: NO transformation of any kind has been applied. Values are raw pg/mL.

label: p-tau217 assay/source identifier
recommended_downstream_handling: documentation only

label: Plasma GFAP
derivation: raw assay at the anchor draw
missing_value_rule: missing for 43 of 410 (ALL from -4/-5 sentinel removal)
recommended_downstream_handling: the CHEAPEST secondary addition: 361 complete cases retaining ALL 85 events
known_caveat: adding it loses 40 participants but ZERO events

label: Plasma NfL
derivation: raw assay at the anchor draw
recommended_downstream_handling: not in the pre-specified secondary set; same coverage as GFAP

label: Plasma Abeta-42
derivation: raw assay at the anchor draw
recommended_downstream_handling: ratio NUMERATOR — must be preserved alongside the ratio

label: Plasma Abeta-40
derivation: raw assay at the anchor draw
recommended_downstream_handling: ratio DENOMINATOR — must be preserved alongside the ratio
known_caveat: 0 zero and 0 non-positive denominators in the cohort

label: Abeta-42/Abeta-40 ratio (recomputed)
derivation: abeta42 / abeta40 — BOTH components come from the SAME raw plasma row (same participant, draw, date and platform), so a compatibility mismatch is structurally impossible
missing_value_rule: non-null iff both components are valid and the denominator > 0; 408 of 410 valid (84 events)
recommended_downstream_handling: complete-case model: 399 participants / 83 events
known_caveat: 0 undefined and 0 infinite ratios. Raw components are preserved.

label: Abeta-42/Abeta-40 ratio as supplied by the vendor
recommended_downstream_handling: QC cross-check only — do NOT substitute for ratio_ab42_ab40
known_caveat: agrees with the recomputed ratio to 5e-05 (rounding)

label: CDR Sum of Boxes (global severity / staging)
derivation: nearest measurement within +/-90d of the anchor (CDVERSION in {1,2})
timing_rule: NEAREST within +/-90d — MAY FALL AFTER THE ANCHOR
recommended_downstream_handling: NOT approved as a primary predictor. Complete-case cost is severe: 314 participants but only 50 of 85 events.
known_caveat: 15 values were measured AFTER the anchor (max +83d) -> potential leakage. Restricting to on-or-before costs 0 events (51->51). -1 'not-done' codes exist in the CDR family and are NOT covered by the -4/-5 sentinel rule (none reached v1).

label: Date of the aligned CDR visit
derivation: date of the selected CDR measurement
recommended_downstream_handling: use to audit cognitive timing

label: CDR date minus anchor date
recommended_downstream_handling: POSITIVE values = measured AFTER the blood draw (leakage risk)

label: MMSE total score (global cognition)
derivation: nearest measurement within +/-90d of the anchor
recommended_downstream_handling: NOT approved as a primary predictor. Best event retention of the cognitive options (354 participants / 81 events).
known_caveat: 17 values measured AFTER the anchor (max +83d). Restricting to on-or-before costs 0 events (82->82). 14 raw '-1' not-done codes exist in MMSE and are NOT covered by the sentinel rule (none reached v1).

label: Date of the aligned MMSE visit
recommended_downstream_handling: use to audit cognitive timing

label: MMSE date minus anchor date
recommended_downstream_handling: POSITIVE = measured AFTER the blood draw

label: MoCA total score (global cognition)
derivation: nearest measurement within +/-90d of the anchor
recommended_downstream_handling: NOT approved as a primary predictor. Most expensive cognitive option: 313 participants but only 41 of 85 events.
known_caveat: 24 values measured AFTER the anchor (max +49d).

label: Date of the aligned MoCA visit
recommended_downstream_handling: use to audit cognitive timing

label: MoCA date minus anchor date
recommended_downstream_handling: POSITIVE = measured AFTER the blood draw

label: FAQ total score (FUNCTIONAL status, not cognition)
derivation: nearest measurement within +/-90d of the anchor
recommended_downstream_handling: NOT approved as a primary predictor. Best coverage (370 participants / 81 events) but the highest leakage.
known_caveat: 51 values measured AFTER the anchor (max +88d) — the highest of any cognitive variable. 32 raw '-1' not-done codes exist in FAQ and are NOT covered by the sentinel rule (none reached v1). Measures FUNCTION, not cognition.

label: Date of the aligned FAQ visit
recommended_downstream_handling: use to audit cognitive timing

label: FAQ date minus anchor date
recommended_downstream_handling: POSITIVE = measured AFTER the blood draw

label: SURVIVAL EVENT — dementia observed
derivation: 1 if a dementia diagnosis (code 3) occurs STRICTLY AFTER the anchor; 0 if censored
missing_value_rule: never missing in the primary cohort (participants without usable post-anchor follow-up are EXCLUDED from the survival cohort, never coded 0)
recommended_downstream_handling: THE event column. Use as-is: Surv(time_to_event_or_censor_days, event_indicator).
known_caveat: 0 does NOT mean 'stable' — it means 'not yet observed to progress'.

label: Date of the first post-anchor dementia diagnosis
derivation: FIRST dementia diagnosis strictly after the anchor
recommended_downstream_handling: provenance only — the duration column is already derived from it
known_caveat: do not recompute durations from dates; use the duration column

label: Source row of the event diagnosis
derivation: 0-based positional index of the record in the raw CSV as delivered, stamped immediately after read_csv and BEFORE any filtering/sorting/dedup
recommended_downstream_handling: trace the event to the raw DXSUM CSV

label: Native ADNI row id of the event diagnosis
recommended_downstream_handling: alternative trace key

label: Date of the last qualifying post-anchor non-dementia visit
derivation: LAST post-anchor CN/MCI diagnosis when no dementia occurs
missing_value_rule: empty for participants with an event
recommended_downstream_handling: provenance only
known_caveat: censoring is ADMINISTRATIVE (last clinical visit) — no death/loss-to-follow-up table exists, so censoring may be informative

label: Source row of the censoring diagnosis
derivation: 0-based positional index of the record in the raw CSV as delivered, stamped immediately after read_csv and BEFORE any filtering/sorting/dedup
recommended_downstream_handling: trace the censor to the raw DXSUM CSV
known_caveat: when this EQUALS anchor_dx_src_row, the participant's entire follow-up is the anchor-matching interval (74 such participants; median 12 days)

label: Native ADNI row id of the censoring diagnosis
recommended_downstream_handling: alternative trace key

label: Event-or-censor date
recommended_downstream_handling: provenance only

label: SURVIVAL DURATION — days from anchor to event or censor
timing_rule: from the anchor (t = 0) to the event or the censor
missing_value_rule: never missing in the primary cohort
recommended_downstream_handling: THE duration column. Use as-is: Surv(time_to_event_or_censor_days, event_indicator).
known_caveat: range 1 - 3694 days (median 425). 62 survival participants have <=30 days — legitimate censored observations, NOT 'stable'.

label: Duration in months (convenience)
missing_value_rule: never missing in the primary cohort
recommended_downstream_handling: prefer the DAYS column for modeling; this is for reporting
known_caveat: derived from days — do not model both

label: Number of post-anchor diagnosis visits
derivation: count of dated diagnoses strictly after the anchor
recommended_downstream_handling: a proxy for follow-up intensity

label: Why the participant was censored
allowed_values: 'last non-dementia (CN/MCI) follow-up' or empty for events
recommended_downstream_handling: documentation only

label: Participant reverted to CN at some post-anchor visit
recommended_downstream_handling: NOT an exclusion; informational

label: In the broad anchor cohort (A)
derivation: TRUE for every row of every frozen cohort file
recommended_downstream_handling: constant within this file

label: In the survival-follow-up cohort (B)
recommended_downstream_handling: constant within the primary file

label: In the primary complete-case cohort (C)
derivation: followup_eligible AND entry_age AND APOE4_COUNT AND ptau217
recommended_downstream_handling: constant TRUE within the primary file — it IS the inclusion rule

label: Deepest cohort reached
recommended_downstream_handling: constant within this file

label: Stage at which the participant was excluded
allowed_values: ALWAYS EMPTY in the primary file
derivation: empty because every row of this file was INCLUDED
missing_value_rule: always empty here (reads back as NaN)
recommended_downstream_handling: see the exclusion log for excluded participants
known_caveat: empty by construction in the primary cohort

label: Why the participant was excluded
allowed_values: ALWAYS EMPTY in the primary file
derivation: empty because every row of this file was INCLUDED
missing_value_rule: always empty here (reads back as NaN)
recommended_downstream_handling: see mci_survival_exclusion_log_v1.tsv
known_caveat: empty by construction

label: entry_age is nonmissing
recommended_downstream_handling: constant TRUE in the primary file

label: APOE4_COUNT is nonmissing
recommended_downstream_handling: constant TRUE in the primary file

label: ptau217 is nonmissing
recommended_downstream_handling: constant TRUE in the primary file

label: gfap is nonmissing
recommended_downstream_handling: use to build the +GFAP secondary cohort without touching the raw data

label: nfl is nonmissing
recommended_downstream_handling: use to build an +NfL cohort

label: Abeta42, Abeta40 and the ratio are all nonmissing
recommended_downstream_handling: use to build the +Abeta42/Abeta40 secondary cohort

label: Dementia diagnosis ON the anchor date
derivation: count of dementia diagnoses on the anchor date
recommended_downstream_handling: NOT AN EXCLUSION (Build 1). 0 for every participant — structurally impossible, since a same-day dementia would be the nearest diagnosis and the draw could not match as MCI.
known_caveat: no human review pending — the count is zero

label: Number of dementia diagnoses BEFORE the anchor
derivation: count of dementia diagnoses strictly before the anchor
recommended_downstream_handling: NOT AN EXCLUSION (generated in Build 1, sampled for review in Build 2). HUMAN ADJUDICATION IS PENDING.
known_caveat: 8 participants have >0 (4 of them are in the primary cohort). A post-anchor dementia in these people may be RE-DOCUMENTATION of prevalent dementia rather than incident progression.

label: FLAG: any dementia diagnosis on or before the anchor
recommended_downstream_handling: NOT AN AUTOMATIC EXCLUSION (Build 1 generated it; Build 2 routed all 8 to human review). HUMAN ADJUDICATION IS PENDING. Do not exclude these participants without a completed, recorded team adjudication.
known_caveat: TRUE for 8 participants (4 in the primary cohort, 1 of which carries an event). Excluding all 8 would give a primary cohort of ~397 with ~84 events — a SCENARIO, not a decision.

label: FLAG: event/censor was not strictly after the anchor
recommended_downstream_handling: NOT AN EXCLUSION FLAG in practice — FALSE for every participant; no human review pending.
known_caveat: 0 occurrences; every follow-up duration is strictly positive
